claimText: The James Webb Space Telescope (JWST) was launched jointly by NASA, the European Space Agency (ESA), and the Canadian Space Agency.
evidenceExcerpt: Launched jointly by NASA, ESA and Canadian Space Agency.
examUse: Statement-based question identifying the collaborating space agencies.

claimText: The James Webb Space Telescope (JWST) functions as an orbiting infrared observatory.
examUse: Statement-based question on the primary electromagnetic spectrum observed by JWST.

claimText: JWST orbits the Sun at the second Lagrange point (L2), approximately 1.5 million kilometers away from Earth.
evidenceExcerpt: Orbits the Sun 1.5 million kilometers away from the Earth at second Lagrange point or L2.
examUse: Statement-based question on the specific orbit and Lagrange point of JWST.
distractorHooks: Orbits the Earth instead of the Sun

claimText: The James Webb Space Telescope (JWST) discovered biosignatures on the exoplanet K2-18 b.
evidenceExcerpt: Cambridge Scientists discovered Biosignatures on Exoplanet K2-18 b, using James Webb Space Telescope (JWST)
examUse: Match-the-following or statement-based question on recent space discoveries.
